changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: Shared sync-job execution record across every integration kind.

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.sql: CREATE INDEX ix_sync_job_connection_created ON sync_job (connection_id, created_at DESC)

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: One-active-job-per-connection guard: race-proof backstop for the service-level check. A DataIntegrityViolationException from this index is translated to the same idempotent- absorb response as the service-level guard (SyncJobService.beginJob).
changeSet.sql: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ux_sync_job_active ON sync_job (connection_id) WHERE status IN ('PENDING', 'RUNNING')

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: Webhook-liveness ("last event processed") watermark, one row per connection — no counters, no history. Written by ConnectionActivityRecorder from the NATS consumer's post-dispatch hook, throttled to at most one write per connection per 15s. Read side feeds SyncStatusService (core-owned data, never populated by per-vendor providers).

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich

preConditions.onFailMessage: connection ownership lookup constraint already present

changeSet.comment: Provide the composite connection key used by the sync-job ownership constraint.

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich

preConditions.onFailMessage: sync-job connection ownership constraint already present

changeSet.comment: Ensure a sync job's connection belongs to the same workspace.
changeSet.sql: ALTER TABLE sync_job ADD CONSTRAINT fk_sync_job_connection_workspace FOREIGN KEY (connection_id, workspace_id) REFERENCES connection (id, workspace_id);

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: Tombstone marker for an issue/pull request that no longer exists upstream, set by the RECONCILIATION deletion sweep (GitHubDeletionSweepService) and by the issues.transferred webhook. A tombstone rather than a hard DELETE because feedback.artifact_id, observation.artifact_id (NOT NULL) and activity_event.target_id reference issue rows by bare id with no FK — a hard delete orphans them silently. Keeping the row preserves those lookups. Read scope is narrow on purpose: only the sync counts and the sweep's own listing filter "deleted_at IS NULL" today; product read surfaces still show tombstoned rows. See Issue.deletedAt for the full accounting.

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: Serves the per-repository live counts the admin sync UI renders (IssueRepository.count{Issues,PullRequests}GroupedByRepositoryIds) and the sweep's local-number listing. Partial on the tombstone predicate because those are exactly the readers that filter on it, and tombstones are the rare case.
changeSet.sql: CREATE INDEX ix_issue_repository_live ON issue (repository_id, issue_type) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich
changeSet.comment: Stable provider repository id (GitHub repository.id / GitLab project.id) on the monitor row. The per-workspace NATS consumer filter and the periodic reconciliation both key on the mutable name_with_owner today; a rename/transfer rewrites that name upstream, after which events publish under the new name (matching no filter → silently ACK-dropped) and the name-keyed reconciliation can no longer resolve the repo. native_id is the stable identity used to re-key name_with_owner instead. Nullable: legacy rows (and PAT rows whose repo is not yet resolved) are backfilled lazily on the next sync that resolves the repository.

changeSet.author: FelixTJDietrich

preConditions.onFailMessage: repository_to_monitor sync cursor columns not present

changeSet.comment:
issue_sync_cursor / pull_request_sync_cursor belong to the historical backfill alone, which paginates CREATED_AT DESC. An earlier incremental sync path (GitHubIssueSyncService / GitHubPullRequestSyncService, via GithubDataSyncService) also checkpointed into these same columns while paginating UPDATED_AT DESC and cleared them only on a clean finish, so any run that aborted mid-pagination (rate limit, restart, transport failure) could leave an UPDATED_AT-ordered cursor behind. GitHubHistoricalBackfillService reads that column back as its resume offset, so backfill can resume a CREATED_AT-ordered walk from an offset that means nothing in that ordering, page to the end, and mark itself complete having permanently skipped a window of history — the skipped items are older than lastIssuesSyncedAt, so the incremental "since" window never picks them up either.
This migration distrusts every cursor that is not provably backfill's own. A poisoned cursor is indistinguishable from a legitimate backfill checkpoint, so it clears both columns wherever that kind's backfill has not yet completed; backfill then restarts that repository from the top. That is safe — backfill upserts idempotently — and costs one re-pagination. Rows whose backfill already completed are left alone: there is nothing left to resume, so the stale cursor is never read and re-paginating a finished repository would be pure cost.
The completeness predicate mirrors RepositoryToMonitor#isIssueBackfillComplete / #isPullRequestBackfillComplete exactly: initialized (high water mark set) AND either the repository had no items (high water mark 0) or the checkpoint walked down to 0.
